rationale: Lens = scope. My round-1 and round-2 verdicts were both APPROVE with empty concrete_changes_required, so I have nothing to mark ADDRESSED/PARTIAL/MISSED on my side; the round-3 fix is tightly scoped to the single remaining R6 hard_block. Verified: (1) commit ade250ef touches EXACTLY the 4 in-scope files — docsync.go (the fix), divergence.go (comment only), divergence_test.go (regression), workflow/architecture.md (doc-sync note). No Bead-6 surface: no skills/plugins, internal/setup, testdata/fixtures, or ADR-0039 in the round-3 delta OR the full base..head diff (the only layout_classifier_test.go hit is the round-2 file, not new). (2) The r2(03ab678e)→r3 delta IS exactly those 4 files, so the 445 round-1 renames and all round-2 fixes are undisturbed; the only re-touched move-tree path is the workflow-owned architecture.md note. (3) The isDocFile change is the minimal in-scope remedy: an exact-name rootOperatorDocs set {CLAUDE.md,AGENTS.md,README.md,BENCH-MOVED.md} that actually TIGHTENS the prior strings.HasPrefix(CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md) match — no over-match letting real source escape; the test's internal/foo.go negative guard confirms it. README.md+BENCH-MOVED.md are the exact paths R6 flagged. go test green; gofmt/vet clean. Required for Bead 5's own complete, so acceptable here.

findings.issue: Commit ade250ef changes exactly 4 files (internal/validate/docsync.go, divergence.go, divergence_test.go, .mindspec/domains/workflow/architecture.md). git show --name-only confirms zero Bead-6 surface (skills/plugins, internal/setup, testdata/fixtures, ADR-0039) and no rename revisited. No creep beyond closing the adr-divergence-unowned hard_block.

diff --name-only 03ab678e..ade250ef equals exactly the 4 round-3 files, so the round-1 move (445 renames, R1-round-1 APPROVE) and the round-2 fixes (BENCH-MOVED links + isProcessArtifact, R1-round-2 APPROVE) are wholly undisturbed. The only move-tree path re-touched is workflow/architecture.md, gaining a doc-sync note for the rootOperatorDocs set — a workflow-domain doc this bead owns; structure/content intact.

The fix replaces strings.HasPrefix(path,"CLAUDE.md")/("AGENTS.md") with an EXACT-name map rootOperatorDocs={CLAUDE.md,AGENTS.md,README.md,BENCH-MOVED.md}. This is a tightening (exact vs prefix), not an over-match: a real source-adjacent top-level .md or a nested README.md is NOT classified as a doc and stays governed. TestRootOperatorDocsNotUnowned asserts both positive classification (4 docs) AND a negative guard (internal/foo.go stays source/unowned, surfaces as the only finding) — proving the lane is not silenced wholesale. Set is consistent with internal/layout DefaultRootDocs + internal/doctor movedTreeRootDocs (the comment honestly notes CLAUDE.md/BENCH-MOVED.md additions). Minimal remedy required by Bead 5's own complete; acceptable in-scope.

go test ./internal/validate/... (TestRootOperatorDocsNotUnowned + TestProcessArtifactsFiltered) PASS; gofmt -l reports nothing on the 3 .go files; go vet ./internal/validate/... clean. README.md + BENCH-MOVED.md confirmed present in the full base..head diff, i.e. the exact paths R6 flagged are the ones the fix now classifies as docs.

My standing round-1/2 note: DOCS-LAYOUT.md ASCII tree + internal/instruct/templates/spec.md stale .mindspec/docs/* path tokens remain Bead-6 static-text reconciliation. Round-3 correctly did NOT pull that forward. Not a Bead-5 blocker.
